---
title: "Leisure Activities - Meths & Stats"
shorttitle: "meths & stats"
author:
  - name: Melissa Senkova Schneiderova
    corresponding: true
    affiliations:
      - name: PVSPS
        department: OVV
        city: Prague
  - name: Josef Mana
    corresponding: false
    affiliations:
      - name: PVSPS
        department: OVV
        city: Prague
  - name: Jiri Lukavsky
    corresponding: false
    affiliations:
      - name: PVSPS
        department: OVV
        city: Prague
  - name: Klara Dadova
    corresponding: false
    affiliations:
      - name: PVSPS
        department: OVV
        city: Prague
  - name: Radek Trnka
    corresponding: false
    affiliations:
      - name: PVSPS
        department: OVV
        city: Prague
  - name: Zuzana Ticha
    corresponding: false
    affiliations:
      - name: PVSPS
        department: OVV
        city: Prague
  - name: Iveta Vojtechova
    corresponding: false
    affiliations:
      - name: PVSPS
        department: OVV
        city: Prague
  - name: Hana Georgi
    corresponding: false
    affiliations:
      - name: PVSPS
        department: OVV
        city: Prague
format:
  apaquarto-html: default
  apaquarto-pdf:
    documentmode: man
bibliography: references.bib
echo: false
warning: false
---

```{r}
#| label: envir

library(targets)
library(tidyverse)
library(gt)
library(rstan) # for stan_version()
library(gtExtras)
library(papaja)

upstore <- tar_path_store()

tar_source()
theme_set(theme_minimal())
```

# Methods

## Analysis

### Leisure activities across lifespan

The distribution of self-reported leisure activities across the lifespan from RETROS were described in three ways: (i) as counts of activity type per participant, (ii) as the number of participants reporting at least one activity of each activity type, and (iii) as a time-series of expected means of self-reported intensity ratings.

Counts of RETROS activity types were described by their means±standard deviations separately for the SA and non-SA groups, stratified by modality (physical, mental, both). Null hypotheses of equal means and stochastical uniformity of SA versus non-SA activity type count distributions were tested using the *t*-test with Welch modification to the degrees of freedom and the Mann-Whitney U test, respectively. Effect sizes of the differences between the SA and non-SA means and distributions were described by Cohen's *d* and the Vargha and Delaney A (VD.A). Furthermore, a set of linear regressions with each activity type as the outcome and the additive effect of SA status, age in years, and education level was fitted to explore difference in counts between SA and non-SA participants of comparable age and education level. The decision threshold for rejecting a null hypothesis was set at p < .05.

The number of participants reporting at least one activity belonging to a given activity type was described by contingency tables of activity types (rows) and SA status (SA, non-SA, columns) separately for each modality (physical, mental). Within each modality, Pearson's $\chi^2$ test of the null hypothesis that SA status and activity type are independent of each other was performed. The strength of the association between SA status and activity type was described using Cramérs' V, as implemented in the "rcompanion" package [@rcompanion]. The decision threshold for rejecting a null hypothesis was set at *p* < .05.

Finally, the expected means of self-reported intensities of single leisure activities across the lifetime were modelled by a Bayesian Generalized Linear Mixed Model (GLMM), with time (half-decades of adult life) as a monotonic predictor [@bürkner2020]. In the model, self-reported intensity of leisure activities was regressed on the half-decade of life (monotonic ordered predictor), SA status, modality, and their two- and three-way interactions on the group-level as well as correlated participant- and activity type-specific intercepts and slopes across half-decades. Posterior draws taken from this model were described by their medians and 95% equal tailed posterior intervals (ETIs). Moreover, pairwise comparisons between expected intensity conditional on half-decades of life, SA status, and modality were directly compared and evaluated using the probability of direction (*pd*) index. All pairwise comparisons estimates are denoted as $\delta_{x-y}$ throughout the article. The computer code used in the data analysis can be accessed at <https://github.com/josefmana/cosactiw-leisact.git>.

### Recent engagement in leisure activities

Recent leisure activities were analysed using *t*-tests to compare both groups in various aspects of their mental activities (number of activities, total time per week, number of activity occasions per week) or social activities (VLS-ALQ subscales regarding private and public social life). We report Cohen's *d* as the effect size measure and used the $\chi^2$ test to test differences in proportions (physical activity expressed as passing WHO recommendations). We used a regression analysis with age (continuous) and education (2 levels) as moderators to account for potential effects of these factors. The results with *p*-values < .05 are considered significant.

# Results

```{r}
#| label: cobra-init
#| output: false

# Load all COBRA-related targets produced by target_cobra
tar_load(c(
  info_data,
  cobra_analysis,
  cobra_regressions,
  cobra_activity_tests,
  physical_activity_analysis,
  social_activity_analysis
),
store = upstore)

# Convenience aliases so downstream code stays readable
dd  <- cobra_analysis$data  # per-participant summary (n_activity, hours, times)
cad <- cobra_analysis$cad   # long-format activity records
activities <- sort(cobra_analysis$activities)

# rnd(): one-decimal formatting alias for rprint() (used throughout inline text)
rnd <- \(x, n = 1) rprint(x, d = n)
# rndci() is sourced from utils.R via tar_source()
# subtract(): subtracts compenents of a vector of length 2 (for in-text t-test differences)
subtract <- \(x) x[[1]] - x[[2]]

tt_age <- apa::t_test(dd$age[dd$sa], dd$age[!dd$sa])
tt_age_diff <- rprint(subtract(tt_age$estimate), 1)
tt_age_diff_ci <- sprintf(
  "[%.1f, %.1f]",
  round(tt_age$conf.int[[1]], 1),
  round(tt_age$conf.int[[2]], 1)
)

edu_prop <- table(dd$sa, dd$edu2) |> prop.table(margin = 1)
```

Based on our criteria, `r sum(info_data$sa)` participants were classified as SA (`r (sum(info_data$sa) / nrow(info_data) * 100) |> rnd()`%) and `r sum(!info_data$sa)` participants as non-SA (`r (sum(!info_data$sa) / nrow(info_data) * 100) |> rnd()`%). The data of one participant were incomplete. SuperAgers were slightly younger than the non-SuperAgers (`r mean(dd$age[dd$sa]) |> rnd()` vs `r mean(dd$age[!dd$sa]) |> rnd()`, mean difference = `r tt_age_diff` years, 95% CI `r tt_age_diff_ci`; `r tt_age |> apa::t_apa(print = FALSE, format = "rmarkdown")`), and being an SA was not associated with education level (`r rprint(100 * edu_prop[1, 2], 1)`% vs `r rprint(100 * edu_prop[2, 2], 1)`% higher education in SA vs non-SA respectively; `r table(dd$sa, dd$edu2) |> chisq.test() |> apa::chisq_apa(print = FALSE, format = "rmarkdown")`). 

## Leisure activities

```{r}
#| label: counts-results

la_counts <- tar_read(counts_comparisons_table,      store = upstore)
la_supp   <- tar_read(counts_comparisons_table_supp, store = upstore)
la_regres <- tar_read(counts_regressions_table,      store = upstore)

tar_load(chisquares, store = upstore)
tar_load(cross_tables, store = upstore)

fmt_msd <- function(x) {
  sub(")", "", sub("(", "\u00b1 ", x, fixed = TRUE), fixed = TRUE)
}

beta <- sub(
  pattern     = "-",
  replacement = "",
  x = with(la_regres, get('b_safnon-Superager')[category == 'flexibility_health_exercise'])
)

beta_CI <-
  sub( "-", "", with(la_regres, `CI_safnon-Superager`[category == "flexibility_health_exercise"]) ) |>
  strsplit(",", fixed = TRUE) |>
  unlist()

beta_CI <- paste0(
  "[", sub( "-", "", sub("]", "", beta_CI[2], fixed = TRUE)),
  ", ", sub("[", "", beta_CI[1], fixed = TRUE),
  "]"
)

```

**Counts of activity types.** Counts of activities per participant in SA and non-SA group and their comparison are presented in @tbl-la-counts (see @tbl-la-counts-supp for non-parametric statistics). No tested null hypothesis was rejected. The only notable pattern was relatively higher number of self-reported activities from the "flexibility and mind-body exercise" activity type by SA (`r fmt_msd(with(la_counts, SA[category == 'flexibility_health_exercise']))`) compared to non-SA (`r fmt_msd(with(la_counts, nonSA[category == 'flexibility_health_exercise']))`) participants (*t*(`r with(la_counts, df[category == 'flexibility_health_exercise'])`) = `r with(la_counts, t[category == 'flexibility_health_exercise'])`, *p* = `r with(la_counts, p[category == 'flexibility_health_exercise'])`, *d* = `r with(la_counts, cohens_d[category == 'flexibility_health_exercise'])`). Similar results were observed in the regression analyses whereby after adjusting for age and education, SA participants were expected to report `r beta` (95% CI `r beta_CI`) activities more than non-SA participants (see @tbl-la-regres).

```{r}
#| label: tbl-la-counts

la_counts |>
  mutate(
    category = if_else(category == "-", "all", category),
    across(
      .cols = all_of(names(la_counts)[5:ncol(la_counts)]), # manually select all stats-relating columns
      .fns = \(x) if_else(nonSA == "-" | SA == "-", "-", x)
    )
  ) |>
  select(-t, -df) |>
  gt_apa(
    grp = "type",
    title = "Description and statistical comparison of activity counts in SA and non-SA groups"
  ) |>
  tab_spanner(columns = "SA",    label = "Superagers") |>
  tab_spanner(columns = "nonSA", label = "non-Superagers") |>
  cols_label(
    category        ~ "Activity type",
    ends_with("SA") ~ "Mean (SD)",
    diff_ci         ~ md("Difference [95% CI]"),
    cohens_d        ~ md("*d*"),
    p               ~ md("*p*")
  )
```

**Number of participants reporting an activity.** The overall distribution of the number of participants engaging in at least one activity from each activity type is presented in @tbl-la-nums. Based on the data, the null hypothesis of activity type and SA status independence was not rejected for either physical ($\chi^2$(`r chisquares['physical', 'df']`) = `r chisquares['physical', 'chisq']`, *p* = `r chisquares['physical', 'p']`, Cramér's *V* = `r chisquares['physical', 'cramer_v']`) or mental ($\chi^2$(`r chisquares['mental', 'df']`) = `r chisquares['mental', 'chisq']`, *p* = `r chisquares['mental', 'p']`, Cramér's *V* = `r chisquares['mental', 'cramer_v']`) activities.

```{r}
#| label: tbl-la-nums

cross_tables |>
  select(-ends_with("SA")) |>
  relocate(nonSA_perc, .before = SA_perc) |>
  mutate(Type = capitalise(Type)) |>
  gt_apa(
    grp = "Type",
    title = "Number of participants reporting at least one mental or physical activity of each type"
  ) |>
  cols_align(
    columns = -c(1:2),
    align = "center"
  ) |>
  cols_label(
    Category ~ "Activity type",
    SA_perc ~ "Superagers",
    nonSA_perc ~ "non-Superagers"
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: lmm

exp     <- tar_read(posterior_expectations, store = upstore)
pwc     <- tar_read(posterior_contrasts, store = upstore)
fit     <- tar_read(intensities_loglinear_regressions, store = upstore)$ordered_time
rhatmax <- ubound(max(brms::rhat(fit)), 3)
```

**Intensity ratings.** Posterior expected intensity ratings predicted by the model are summarised in @fig-cond-means. According to the model and data, self-reported intensity of physical activities decreases with age whereas self-reported intensity of mental activities increases with age (left panel of @fig-cond-means). These modality-dependent opposing tendencies seem to be invariant to the SA status (right panel of @fig-cond-means). Pairwise comparisons of expected intensity rating between life half-decades conditional on SA status and activity modality are presented in @tbl-pair-comps. The model predicts that average intensity ratings of physical activities decrease between 85 and 30 years of age by `r sub( '-', '', sub(' .*', '', subset(pwc, diff == '85 - 30' & actype == 'physical' & SA == 'SA')$Estimate_logexp) )` points in SA (95% ETI `r paste( strsplit(subset(pwc, diff == '85 - 30' & actype == 'physical' & SA == 'SA')$Estimate_logexp, ' ')[[1]][2:3], collapse = ' ')`, *pd* = `r subset(pwc, diff == '85 - 30' & actype == 'physical' & SA == 'nonSA')$pd_logexp`) and by `r sub( '-', '', sub(' .*', '', subset(pwc, diff == '85 - 30' & actype == 'physical' & SA == 'nonSA')$Estimate_logexp) )` points in non-SA (95% ETI `r paste( strsplit(subset(pwc, diff == '85 - 30' & actype == 'physical' & SA == 'nonSA')$Estimate_logexp, ' ')[[1]][2:3], collapse = ' ')`, *pd* = `r subset(pwc, diff == '85 - 30' & actype == 'physical' & SA == 'nonSA')$pd_logexp`) participants. On the other, it predicts that average intensity ratings of mental activities increase between 85 and 30 years of age by `r sub(' .*', '', subset(pwc, diff == '85 - 30' & actype == 'mental' & SA == 'SA')$Estimate_logexp)` points in SA (95% ETI `r paste( strsplit(subset(pwc, diff == '85 - 30' & actype == 'mental' & SA == 'SA')$Estimate_logexp, ' ')[[1]][2:3], collapse = ' ')`, *pd* = `r subset(pwc, diff == '85 - 30' & actype == 'mental' & SA == 'nonSA')$pd_logexp`) and by `r sub(' .*', '', subset(pwc, diff == '85 - 30' & actype == 'mental' & SA == 'nonSA')$Estimate_logexp)` points in non-SA (95% ETI `r paste( strsplit(subset(pwc, diff == '85 - 30' & actype == 'mental' & SA == 'nonSA')$Estimate_logexp, ' ')[[1]][2:3], collapse = ' ')`, *pd* = `r subset(pwc, diff == '85 - 30' & actype == 'mental' & SA == 'nonSA')$pd_logexp`) participants. According to the model, the difference between these trends in mental and physical activity ratings evolution is non-zero with high posterior probability on the level of almost certainly existing association in both SA ($\delta_{physical-mental}$ = `r sub(' .*', '', subset(pwc, diff == '85 - 30' & diff2 == 'physical - mental' & SA == 'SA')$Estimate_logexp)`, 95% ETI `r paste( strsplit(subset(pwc, diff == '85 - 30' & diff2 == 'physical - mental' & SA == 'SA')$Estimate_logexp, ' ')[[1]][2:3], collapse = ' ')`, *pd* = `r subset(pwc, diff == '85 - 30' & diff2 == 'physical - mental' & SA == 'SA')$pd_logexp`) and non-SA ($\delta_{physical-mental}$ = `r sub(' .*', '', subset(pwc, diff == '85 - 30' & diff2 == 'physical - mental' & SA == 'nonSA')$Estimate_logexp)`, 95% ETI `r paste( strsplit(subset(pwc, diff == '85 - 30' & diff2 == 'physical - mental' & SA == 'nonSA')$Estimate_logexp, ' ')[[1]][2:3], collapse = ' ')`, *pd* = `r subset(pwc, diff == '85 - 30' & diff2 == 'physical - mental' & SA == 'nonSA')$pd_logexp`) participants. On the other hand, these trends do not significantly differ between SA and non-SA participants in either mental ($\delta_{SA-nonSA}$ = `r sub(' .*', '', subset(pwc, diff == '85 - 30' & diff2 == 'SA - nonSA' & actype == 'mental')$Estimate_logexp)`, 95% ETI `r paste( strsplit(subset(pwc, diff == '85 - 30' & diff2 == 'SA - nonSA' & actype == 'mental')$Estimate_logexp, ' ')[[1]][2:3], collapse = ' ')`, *pd* = `r subset(pwc, diff == '85 - 30' & diff2 == 'SA - nonSA' & actype == 'mental')$pd_logexp`) or physical ($\delta_{SA-nonSA}$ = `r sub(' .*', '', subset(pwc, diff == '85 - 30' & diff2 == 'SA - nonSA' & actype == 'physical')$Estimate_logexp)`, 95% ETI `r paste( strsplit(subset(pwc, diff == '85 - 30' & diff2 == 'SA - nonSA' & actype == 'physical')$Estimate_logexp, ' ')[[1]][2:3], collapse = ' ')`, *pd* = `r subset(pwc, diff == '85 - 30' & diff2 == 'SA - nonSA' & actype == 'physical')$pd_logexp`) activities.

![Three-way interaction between half-decade of life, SuperAging, and activity modality in a model of self-reported leisure activities ratings.](../figures/conditional_means_back_transformed_raw_scale.jpg){#fig-cond-means}

## Recent engagement in leisure activities

**Mental activities.** From the proposed list of eight mental activities, the participants reported on average being involved in `r mean(dd$n_activity) |> rnd()` (SD = `r sd(dd$n_activity) |> rnd()`, median = `r median(dd$n_activity) |> rnd()`). On average, they spent `r mean(dd$total_hours_per_week) |> rnd()` hours in these activities (SD = `r sd(dd$total_hours_per_week) |> rnd()`, median = `r median(dd$total_hours_per_week) |> rnd()`). Combining individual activities with their reported weekly frequency, we calculated how many times participants were involved in the selected mental activities on a weekly basis. On average, they reported `r mean(dd$total_times_per_week) |> rnd()` occasions in these activities (SD = `r sd(dd$total_times_per_week) |> rnd()`, median = `r median(dd$total_times_per_week) |> rnd()`).

SuperAgers reported a slightly larger number of activities (`r mean(dd$n_activity[dd$sa]) |> rnd()` vs `r mean(dd$n_activity[!dd$sa]) |> rnd()`; `r apa::t_test(dd$n_activity[dd$sa], dd$n_activity[!dd$sa]) |> apa::t_apa(print = FALSE, format = "rmarkdown")`). They did not differ in the number of hours spent in these activities (`r mean(dd$total_hours_per_week[dd$sa]) |> rnd()` vs `r mean(dd$total_hours_per_week[!dd$sa]) |> rnd()`; `r apa::t_test(dd$total_hours_per_week[dd$sa], dd$total_hours_per_week[!dd$sa]) |> apa::t_apa(print = FALSE, format = "rmarkdown")`) or the number of weekly occasions (`r mean(dd$total_times_per_week[dd$sa]) |> rnd()` vs `r mean(dd$total_times_per_week[!dd$sa]) |> rnd()`; `r apa::t_test(dd$total_times_per_week[dd$sa], dd$total_times_per_week[!dd$sa]) |> apa::t_apa(print = FALSE, format = "rmarkdown")`). To account for the potential effect of age and education, we used regression models with age and education as covariates. This analysis confirmed a significant difference between SA and non-SA in the number of activities (by `r -cobra_regressions$coef_n_activity["safnon-Superager",] |> rndci(infix = ", 95%% CI ")`), no difference in total time (by `r -cobra_regressions$coef_hours["safnon-Superager",] |> rndci(infix = " ")`). The number of weekly occasions reached significance when corrected for the effect of age and education (by `r -cobra_regressions$coef_times["safnon-Superager",] |> rndci(infix = " ")`).

As an exploratory analysis, we compared the involvement in individual activities using a series of *t*-tests. The only notable pattern was a non-significant preference for card- and board games (`r cobra_activity_tests[[activities[4]]] |> apa::t_apa(print = FALSE, format = "rmarkdown")`; other uncorrected p-values > .300). The average time spent in each activity per week is summarised in @tbl-cobra-activity (see @tbl-cobra-supplemental for parallel non-parametric comparisons).

```{r}
#| label: tbl-cobra-activity

tar_read(cobra_activity_table, store = upstore)
```

**Physical activity.** Majority of the sample passed WHO criteria for sufficient physical activity (N = `r physical_activity_analysis$n_who_pa`, or `r physical_activity_analysis$pct_who_pa |> rnd()`%). Fulfilling the WHO criteria was not associated with SuperAging (`r physical_activity_analysis$chisq |> apa::chisq_apa(print = FALSE, format = "rmarkdown")`).

**Social activity.** We compared SA and non-SA in the intensity of their private and public social activities. We found no differences in either aspect of their social life (VLS-ALQ private, SA vs non-SA: `r social_activity_analysis$means["sa_private"] |> rnd()` vs `r social_activity_analysis$means["nonsa_private"] |> rnd()`; `r social_activity_analysis$private_ttest |> apa::t_apa(print = FALSE, format = "rmarkdown")`; VLS-ALQ public: `r social_activity_analysis$means["sa_public"] |> rnd()` vs `r social_activity_analysis$means["nonsa_public"] |> rnd()`; `r social_activity_analysis$public_ttest |> apa::t_apa(print = FALSE, format = "rmarkdown")`).

# References

# Appendix

```{r}
#| label: tbl-la-counts-supp

la_supp |>
  mutate(
    category = if_else(category == "-", "all", category),
    across(
      .cols = all_of(names(la_supp)[5:ncol(la_supp)]), # manually select all stats-relating columns
      .fns = \(x) if_else(SA_med_range == "-" | nonSA_med_range == "-", "-", x)
    )
  ) |>
  gt_apa(
    grp = "type",
    title = "Description and statistical comparison of activity counts in SA and non-SA groups (non-parametric)"
  ) |>
  tab_spanner(columns = "SA_med_range",    label = "Superagers") |>
  tab_spanner(columns = "nonSA_med_range", label = "non-Superagers") |>
  cols_label(
    category       ~ "Activity type",
    contains("SA") ~ md("Median (min\u2013max)"),
    VDA            ~ md("VD.A"),
    W              ~ md("*W*"),
    p              ~ md("*p*")
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: tbl-la-regres

la_regres |>
  mutate(
    `Activity Type`   = if_else(category == "-", "all", category),
    Intercept         = paste(`b_(Intercept)`,      `CI_(Intercept)`     , sep = " "),
    SuperAging        = paste(`b_safnon-Superager`, `CI_safnon-Superager`, sep = " "),
    Age               = paste(b_age,                 CI_age,               sep = " "),
    `Education level` = paste(b_edu22,               CI_edu22,             sep = " ")
  ) |>
  select(
    type, `Activity Type`,
    nonSA, SA,
    Intercept, SuperAging, Age, `Education level`
  ) |>
  gt_apa(
    grp = "type",
    title = "Regression analysis of activity types as depending on SA status, age and education."
  ) |>
  tab_spanner(columns = SA,    "Superagers") |>
  tab_spanner(columns = nonSA, "non-Superagers") |>
  tab_spanner(
    columns = c(Intercept, SuperAging, Age, `Education level`),
    label = "Regression parameters (b [95% CI])",
    gather = FALSE
  ) |>
  cols_label(ends_with("SA") ~ "Mean (SD)")
```

```{r}
#| label: tbl-pair-comps

pwc |>
  filter(!grepl("[A-Za-z]", diff) ) |>
  filter(is.na(diff2)) |>
  filter(!is.na(SA)) |>
  filter(!is.na(actype)) |>
  mutate(
    actype = paste0(capitalise(actype)," activities"),
    SA = sub("non", "non-", SA)
  ) |>
  select(-ends_with("log"), -timebin, -diff2) |>
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = SA,
    values_from = ends_with("logexp")
  ) |>
  relocate(pd_logexp_SA, .after = Estimate_logexp_SA) |>
  relocate(`pd_logexp_non-SA`, .after = `Estimate_logexp_non-SA`) |>
  gt_apa(
    grp = "actype",
    title = "Pairwise comparisons of self-reported non-seasonal activity intensities across half-decades of life stratified by SuperAging status, and activity modality"
  ) |>
  tab_spanner(columns = ends_with("_SA"), label = "SA") |>
  tab_spanner(columns = ends_with("_non-SA"), label = "non-SA") |>
  cols_align(columns = "diff", align = "left") |>
  cols_align(columns = -2:-1, align = "center") |>
  cols_label(
    diff ~ "Half-decade",
    contains("Estimate") ~ "Estimate [95% ETI]", 
    contains("pd") ~ "pd"
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: tbl-cobra-supplemental

tar_read(cobra_activity_table_supp, store = upstore)
```
